metadata.description: Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM) with weighted WSJF-style priority.

components.name: RBAC
components.description: Role-based access control and permissions matrix

components.name: Content Service
components.description: Content generation, storage, status state machine

components.name: Audit Logging
components.description: Immutable audit trail for actions and changes

components.name: Security
components.description: AppSec controls, rate limiting, secret management

components.name: Data Protection
components.description: Encryption at rest/in transit, PII handling

components.name: Multi-tenant DB
components.description: Tenant isolation with organization_id and RLS

requirements.title: Multi-tenant isolation with organization_id and Row-Level Security (RLS)
requirements.description: Enforce strict tenant data isolation in a single database using organization_id scoping and PostgreSQL RLS policies.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user from Org A, when querying any table, then rows from Org B are never returned.
Given direct ID references, when accessing resources, then RLS denies cross-tenant access.
Given admin tooling, when viewing system data, then cross-tenant views require Super Admin role.

test_cases.title: Strong password policy enforced on signup/change
test_cases.description: Validate password complexity and rejection of weak passwords.
preconditions: Test org exists; signup enabled; password policy configured per spec
steps: Attempt signup with weak password (e.g., 'password1')
Verify inline error shows policy requirements
Sign up with strong password meeting policy
Change password to another strong value from profile/security page
expected_results: Weak password rejected with clear reasons
Strong password accepted; user created/session issued
Password change succeeds; audit entry created

requirements.implementation_notes: Define RLS policies per table; enforce org_id on all queries via middleware/ORM scopes.

requirements.title: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) per permission matrix
requirements.description: Implement roles (Super Admin, Admin, Advisor, Compliance) with least-privilege permissions across all modules.
acceptance_criteria: Given an Advisor, when attempting to publish, then action is blocked until Approved by Compliance/Admin.
Given a Compliance user, when reviewing content, then approve/reject/edit is permitted.
Given an Admin, when managing users, then invite/remove/role-change is permitted.

test_cases.title: Session token security and expiration
test_cases.description: Validate secure tokens, expiration, and refresh behavior.
preconditions: User account exists and can log in
steps: Log in and capture auth cookies/headers
Wait until token TTL elapses
Call a protected API and UI action
Re-authenticate and retry action
expected_results: Tokens are HttpOnly/SameSite/Lax or Strict and secure
Expired token returns 401; UI prompts re-login
After re-auth, action succeeds; prior session invalidated

requirements.implementation_notes: Backend role checks and frontend route visibility are live. Verified with publish-gating.json for advisor restrictions, compliance-review-flow.json for compliance actions, admin-user-lifecycle.json for invite/remove/role-change, and the persona sweep across all role shells.

requirements.title: AI pre-generation compliance guardrails
requirements.description: Apply prohibited terms filtering, required disclosures, and constraints before content generation.
acceptance_criteria: Given a prompt with prohibited terms, when generating, then block or sanitize before send to model.
Given investment content, when generating, then required risk disclosures are included.
Given ICP config, when generating, then ICP-specific rules are applied.

test_cases.title: Password reset flow is secure
test_cases.description: Reset tokens are single-use, time-limited, and invalidated on use.
preconditions: User exists with verified email; email delivery enabled (SendGrid sandbox)
steps: Initiate password reset
Open reset link within validity window; set new strong password
Attempt using the same reset link again
Attempt reset link after expiry window
expected_results: Reset succeeds; audit event recorded
Second attempt fails with invalid/used token
Expired link rejected with secure error; no leakage

requirements.implementation_notes: Pre-generation prohibited-term blocking is verified with ai-guardrails.json. The remaining disclosure and ICP-application acceptance criteria still require a live OpenAI key because this environment is currently configured with a placeholder API key.

requirements.title: Post-generation compliance validation and auto-disclaimers
requirements.description: Scan AI output for violations, auto-insert disclaimers, and flag uncertain content for human review.
acceptance_criteria: Given generated content, when scanned, then violations are flagged and content routed to review.
Given missing disclosure, when detected, then system auto-inserts compliant disclaimer text.
Given uncertain compliance score, when below threshold, then content is forced to Pending Review.

test_cases.title: Permission matrix enforcement across roles
test_cases.description: Verify CRUD and approval rights per role per matrix.
preconditions: Users exist for all roles in same org; sample content present
steps: Advisor attempts to publish without approval
Compliance edits and approves content
Admin views analytics for all users; Advisor views own only
expected_results: Advisor blocked from publishing pre-approval
Compliance can edit/approve; status updates recorded
Admin sees org-wide analytics; Advisor sees own data only

requirements.implementation_notes: Use rule-based scans with configurable thresholds; maintain audit of inserted text.

requirements.title: Compliance workflow enforcement (Draft → Review → Approved/Rejected)
requirements.description: Enforce content state transitions with permissions, notes, and publication gating.
acceptance_criteria: Given Draft content, when Advisor submits, then status changes to Pending Review and notifies Compliance.
Given Rejected content, when Advisor edits, then it can be resubmitted with change notes.
Given Approved content, when publishing, then only permitted roles can publish.

test_cases.title: Row-level security: cross-tenant isolation
test_cases.description: Users cannot access other organizations’ data.
preconditions: Org A and Org B exist; user U_A (Org A) logged in; content and leads exist in both orgs
steps: Directly navigate to Org B resource IDs via URL/API
Attempt search/filter to surface cross-tenant data
Inspect network responses for foreign records
expected_results: All attempts return 403/404 with no data leakage
No foreign records appear; audit entries for denied access

test_cases.title: Analytics visibility respects role and tenant
test_cases.description: Dashboards filter by organization and role permissions.
preconditions: Org A and Org B with data; Admin/Advisor/Compliance users exist
steps: Admin Org A views analytics (org-wide)
Advisor Org A views analytics (own-only)
Cross-tenant URL/API attempts to fetch Org B analytics
expected_results: Admin sees only Org A aggregated data
Advisor sees own metrics only
Cross-tenant access blocked (403/404); no leakage

requirements.implementation_notes: Finite-state review workflow enforced in backend and surfaced in UI. Verified with persona sweep, compliance-review-flow.json, and publish-gating.json. Legacy TCC mappings under FR-005 remain stale and should be normalized in a later traceability cleanup.

requirements.title: Immutable audit trail for content and user actions
requirements.description: Track create/edit/review/approval/publish actions with timestamp, actor, and diffs; records are immutable.
acceptance_criteria: Given any content change, when saved, then an audit record is appended with before/after diffs.
Given a status change, when performed, then actor, timestamp, and reason are recorded.
Given an audit export, when requested, then entries are ordered, complete, and non-editable.

test_cases.title: Pre-generation guardrails block prohibited terms
test_cases.description: Prohibited terms are blocked and explain why.
preconditions: Advisor logged in; prohibited terms list configured
steps: Enter prompt containing guarantees/promissory language
Submit generation request
expected_results: Request blocked or sanitized with rationale referencing FINRA/SEC
Event logged with prompt, user, and rule triggered

test_cases.title: Post-generation validation and disclaimers insertion
test_cases.description: Generated content is scanned; disclaimers auto-inserted or flagged.
preconditions: Advisor logged in; firm-specific disclosures configured
steps: Generate content for investment topic
Review output across channels (LinkedIn, Facebook, Email)
expected_results: Required disclosures appended per channel
If uncertain, content flagged Pending Review with reasons
Validation events captured in audit log

requirements.implementation_notes: Use append-only log with cryptographic hash chaining optional for tamper-evidence.

requirements.title: Compliance export to PDF/Word
requirements.description: Export approved or reviewed content with metadata and audit trail for recordkeeping.
acceptance_criteria: Given an Approved item, when exporting, then PDF/Word includes content, disclosures, and approvals.
Given Pending Review, when exporting, then reviewer notes are included.
Given export, when downloaded, then file is accessible to authorized roles only.

test_cases.title: Post-generation validation and disclaimers insertion
test_cases.description: Generated content is scanned; disclaimers auto-inserted or flagged.
preconditions: Advisor logged in; firm-specific disclosures configured
steps: Generate content for investment topic
Review output across channels (LinkedIn, Facebook, Email)
expected_results: Required disclosures appended per channel
If uncertain, content flagged Pending Review with reasons
Validation events captured in audit log

requirements.implementation_notes: Server-side document generation with stamped metadata and checksums.

requirements.title: Secrets management and token encryption
requirements.description: Encrypt OAuth tokens and API keys at rest; rotate and restrict access via environment vault.
acceptance_criteria: Given stored tokens, when inspected at DB layer, then values are encrypted.
Given key rotation, when executed, then services continue without token leakage.
Given service startup, when loading secrets, then only least-privileged processes can read them.

test_cases.title: Compliance workflow transitions and gating
test_cases.description: Status transitions follow Draft→Pending→Approved/Rejected/Needs Changes→Published.
preconditions: Advisor and Compliance users exist
steps: Advisor creates content and submits for review
Compliance returns Needs Changes with notes
Advisor updates and resubmits
Compliance approves; Admin publishes
expected_results: Invalid transitions blocked; notes retained
Publish possible only after approval
All transitions timestamped with user in audit log

requirements.implementation_notes: Use KMS/Secrets Manager; encrypt columns; implement rotation playbooks.

requirements.title: Encryption in transit and at rest
requirements.description: Enable TLS for all endpoints and database connections; encrypt sensitive data at rest.
acceptance_criteria: Given any API call, when intercepted, then traffic is TLS 1.2+ encrypted.
Given database storage, when querying encryption status, then at-rest encryption is enabled.
Given backups, when created, then backups are encrypted with managed keys.

test_cases.title: Comprehensive audit trail for content lifecycle
test_cases.description: All edits, reviews, approvals, and publications are logged immutably.
preconditions: Content item exists; multiple user roles available
steps: Make edit, change status, approve, publish
Open audit view for the content and export JSON/CSV if available
expected_results: Each action present with timestamp, actor, before/after
No edits to audit entries allowed; read-only view

test_cases.title: Export to PDF/Word for recordkeeping
test_cases.description: Records export contains content, approvals, and disclosures.
preconditions: Approved content exists
steps: From compliance view, export PDF and Word
Open files and inspect contents
expected_results: Exports include final content, disclosures, approval metadata, and audit summary
Files stored/available for download; access controlled

test_cases.title: Compliance notifications emitted and logged
test_cases.description: Notifications sent on submit/approve/reject/changes.
preconditions: Email delivery enabled; in-app notifications visible
steps: Advisor submits content
Compliance approves; then rejects another item
Open notification center and check emails
expected_results: Correct recipients get email + in-app alerts
Notification events present in audit with correlation IDs

test_cases.title: OpenAI usage and cost tracking per user/org
test_cases.description: Usage recorded with content ID correlation and totals.
preconditions: Advisor generates multiple contents in same org
steps: Generate content across channels
Open usage dashboard/logs
expected_results: Tokens and cost attributed to user and org with content correlation IDs
Audit entries include usage metadata

test_cases.title: Data credit purchase, deduction, and audit
test_cases.description: Credits purchased via Stripe; deductions on list import; no negative balances.
preconditions: Org has zero credits; Stripe test mode enabled; Prospect Finder request ready for import
steps: Purchase credit bundle
Submit Prospect Finder request; import list preview and confirm
Verify credits deducted per pricing tier; try import exceeding balance
expected_results: Balance increases post-webhook
Deduction matches record types; audit entries for purchase and deduction
Exceeding balance blocked with clear error; no negative balance

requirements.implementation_notes: Enforce HTTPS, HSTS; enable PG encryption at rest via cloud provider; encrypt S3 objects.

requirements.title: API rate limiting and input validation
requirements.description: Apply per-tenant and per-user rate limits; validate and sanitize inputs to prevent abuse.
acceptance_criteria: Given excessive requests, when limits exceeded, then 429 is returned and logged.
Given invalid input, when submitted, then request is rejected with detailed but safe errors.
Given known bad patterns, when detected, then requests are blocked and alerted.

test_cases.title: Export to PDF/Word for recordkeeping
test_cases.description: Records export contains content, approvals, and disclosures.
preconditions: Approved content exists
steps: From compliance view, export PDF and Word
Open files and inspect contents
expected_results: Exports include final content, disclosures, approval metadata, and audit summary
Files stored/available for download; access controlled

requirements.implementation_notes: Implement token buckets; centralize validation schemas; WAF rules as needed.

requirements.title: Security event logging and monitoring
requirements.description: Log auth failures, permission denials, rate limit hits, and suspicious activities with alerts.
acceptance_criteria: Given repeated auth failures, when threshold reached, then alert is sent to ops channel.
Given privilege escalation attempt, when detected, then event is logged with context.
Given rate limit bursts, when anomalous, then alerts include tenant and IP.

test_cases.title: Encryption at rest and TLS in transit
test_cases.description: Verify TLS enforcement and encrypted sensitive fields.
preconditions: Staging uses valid TLS; DB has encrypted columns for secrets/tokens
steps: Attempt HTTP access; inspect redirect/blocking
Inspect DB for ciphered OAuth token fields (no plaintext)
Intercept traffic with proxy to confirm TLS
expected_results: HTTP blocked or redirected to HTTPS; HSTS present
Secrets/tokens stored encrypted; no plaintext found
All traffic encrypted end-to-end

requirements.implementation_notes: Centralized log aggregation; alert rules; retain logs per compliance policy.

requirements.title: Access logging for PII and sensitive records
requirements.description: Record who viewed or exported PII with purpose and timestamp; enable audit queries.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user views a lead with PII, when accessed, then a view log entry is created.
Given CSV export with PII, when executed, then actor, scope, and reason are logged.
Given audit search, when filtered by user or record, then matching access events are returned.

test_cases.title: PII access logging and least privilege
test_cases.description: Access to PII is logged and restricted by role/tenant.
preconditions: Leads with PII exist; multiple roles available
steps: Advisor views own lead PII
Advisor attempts to view another advisor’s lead
Compliance views org-wide but read-only PII (where applicable)
Review PII access logs
expected_results: Unauthorized access blocked
Every PII read logged with actor/time/object
Compliance access follows policy; no edits unless allowed

requirements.implementation_notes: Tag PII fields; instrument read paths; restrict log visibility by role.

requirements.title: Publish gating: only approved content can be published
requirements.description: Block publishing from Advisor role unless content is Approved by Compliance/Admin.
acceptance_criteria: Given content not Approved, when publish is attempted, then action is denied with reason.
Given Approved content, when published, then publication event is audited.
Given bypass attempts, when detected, then system prevents and logs the attempt.

test_cases.title: Secure storage of API keys and redaction
test_cases.description: API keys only in env; redacted in logs/UI.
preconditions: System configured with OpenAI/SendGrid/Stripe keys
steps: Open settings/admin diagnostics
Trigger error that would include env in logs
Search logs for secrets
expected_results: No secrets displayed in UI or logs; values redacted
No endpoint returns secrets

requirements.implementation_notes: Backend publish endpoints hard-gate non-approved drafts and block non-advisor/admin roles. AC-1 verified with publish-gating.json; AC-2/AC-3 still need explicit audit-path evidence.

requirements.title: Compliance notifications for key events
requirements.description: Send Email and in-app notifications for submission, approval, rejection, and changes needed.
acceptance_criteria: Given content submitted, when event occurs, then Compliance receives Email and in-app alerts.
Given content approved/rejected, when event occurs, then Advisor is notified with details.
Given changes needed, when sent, then Advisor sees notes inline.

test_cases.title: Encryption at rest and TLS in transit
test_cases.description: Verify TLS enforcement and encrypted sensitive fields.
preconditions: Staging uses valid TLS; DB has encrypted columns for secrets/tokens
steps: Attempt HTTP access; inspect redirect/blocking
Inspect DB for ciphered OAuth token fields (no plaintext)
Intercept traffic with proxy to confirm TLS
expected_results: HTTP blocked or redirected to HTTPS; HSTS present
Secrets/tokens stored encrypted; no plaintext found
All traffic encrypted end-to-end

test_cases.title: OAuth tokens encrypted and rotated
test_cases.description: 3rd-party tokens encrypted at rest; rotation supported.
preconditions: LinkedIn/Facebook accounts connected
steps: Connect LinkedIn; inspect DB token field
Force token rotation/refresh
Validate prior token unusable; new token works and remains encrypted
expected_results: Tokens stored encrypted; not readable in plaintext
Rotation invalidates old token; new token functions

test_cases.title: Minimal OAuth scopes and revocation handling
test_cases.description: Only required scopes requested; revocation detected.
preconditions: User connects LinkedIn and Facebook with required scopes
steps: Inspect consent screen scopes
Post a test item; then revoke access at provider
Attempt another post; reconnect flow
expected_results: Only documented scopes requested
After revocation, posting fails gracefully and prompts reconnect
Reconnected account works; tokens encrypted

requirements.implementation_notes: Use SendGrid for email; in-app toast and inbox; configurable notification prefs.

requirements.title: ICP-specific compliance rules configuration
requirements.description: Allow admins to configure firm/ICP-specific disclosures, prohibited terms, and terminology.
acceptance_criteria: Given an ICP config change, when saved, then new rules apply to subsequent generations.
Given firm-specific disclosure text, when configured, then it appears in generated outputs.
Given prohibited list updates, when applied, then pre-checks enforce them.

test_cases.title: API rate limiting enforces quotas
test_cases.description: Excess requests return 429 and are logged.
preconditions: Known per-user/per-IP limits configured
steps: Burst requests to a protected API beyond limit
Observe responses and retry after window
expected_results: 429 with Retry-After header
Requests accepted after cooldown; throttle event logged

requirements.implementation_notes: Store configs per organization and ICP; hot-reload into guardrails service.

requirements.title: Prohibited terms list management and enforcement
requirements.description: Manage customizable lists of prohibited/flagged terms with enforcement in pre/post checks.
acceptance_criteria: Given prohibited terms, when present in prompts, then generation is blocked or sanitized.
Given prohibited terms in outputs, when detected, then content is flagged for review.
Given term updates, when saved, then enforcement takes effect immediately.

test_cases.title: Compliance notifications emitted and logged
test_cases.description: Notifications sent on submit/approve/reject/changes.
preconditions: Email delivery enabled; in-app notifications visible
steps: Advisor submits content
Compliance approves; then rejects another item
Open notification center and check emails
expected_results: Correct recipients get email + in-app alerts
Notification events present in audit with correlation IDs

requirements.implementation_notes: Prompt blocking and immediate enforcement after term updates are verified with ai-guardrails.json and compliance-settings-guardrails.json. Post-generation output flagging is verified with post-generation-flags.json and the advisor__content__drafts__flagged.png screenshot, so the requirement is fully covered without placeholder UI.

requirements.title: Data credits audit trail and deduction logs
requirements.description: Track credit purchases, balances, and per-record deductions with immutable logs.
acceptance_criteria: Given a list import, when credits are deducted, then entries record counts and costs.
Given a credit purchase, when completed, then balance and ledger reflect transaction.
Given audit query, when filtered by org/date, then credit events are returned.

test_cases.title: Stripe webhook signature verification
test_cases.description: Reject invalid signatures; process valid events once.
preconditions: Stripe webhook secret configured; test mode events available
steps: Send webhook with invalid signature
Send valid invoice.paid event
Replay the same valid event (idempotency test)
expected_results: Invalid request 400/401 rejected; no state change
Valid event processed; audit recorded
Replay ignored (idempotent); no duplicate effects

test_cases.title: Data credit purchase, deduction, and audit
test_cases.description: Credits purchased via Stripe; deductions on list import; no negative balances.
preconditions: Org has zero credits; Stripe test mode enabled; Prospect Finder request ready for import
steps: Purchase credit bundle
Submit Prospect Finder request; import list preview and confirm
Verify credits deducted per pricing tier; try import exceeding balance
expected_results: Balance increases post-webhook
Deduction matches record types; audit entries for purchase and deduction
Exceeding balance blocked with clear error; no negative balance

requirements.implementation_notes: Double-entry style ledger; reconcile with Stripe events.

requirements.title: Stripe PCI-safe handling and webhook verification
requirements.description: Do not store card data; verify signed webhooks; enforce least scopes and secure retries.
acceptance_criteria: Given payment methods, when managed, then Stripe-hosted components are used exclusively.
Given webhooks, when received, then signatures are verified and idempotency enforced.
Given webhook replay, when detected, then duplicate effects are prevented.

test_cases.title: Minimal OAuth scopes and revocation handling
test_cases.description: Only required scopes requested; revocation detected.
preconditions: User connects LinkedIn and Facebook with required scopes
steps: Inspect consent screen scopes
Post a test item; then revoke access at provider
Attempt another post; reconnect flow
expected_results: Only documented scopes requested
After revocation, posting fails gracefully and prompts reconnect
Reconnected account works; tokens encrypted

requirements.implementation_notes: Use Stripe Checkout/Elements; verify signatures; store only Stripe IDs and metadata.

requirements.title: OAuth scopes minimization, refresh, and revocation
requirements.description: Request minimal LinkedIn/Facebook scopes; encrypt tokens; support refresh and user-initiated revocation.
acceptance_criteria: Given account connect, when authorizing, then only documented minimal scopes are requested.
Given user disconnect, when triggered, then tokens are revoked and deleted.
Given token expiry, when occurring, then refresh is automatic without user impact.

test_cases.title: OpenAI usage and cost tracking per user/org
test_cases.description: Usage recorded with content ID correlation and totals.
preconditions: Advisor generates multiple contents in same org
steps: Generate content across channels
Open usage dashboard/logs
expected_results: Tokens and cost attributed to user and org with content correlation IDs
Audit entries include usage metadata

requirements.implementation_notes: Document scopes; build disconnect flows; handle provider-specific refresh rules.

requirements.title: Content versioning and history with diffs
requirements.description: Maintain version history for all content edits with ability to compare versions.
acceptance_criteria: Given content edits, when saved, then a new version is created and linked.
Given two versions, when compared, then field-level diffs are shown.
Given rollback, when requested by authorized role, then previous version becomes current and is audited.

test_cases.title: Data credit purchase, deduction, and audit
test_cases.description: Credits purchased via Stripe; deductions on list import; no negative balances.
preconditions: Org has zero credits; Stripe test mode enabled; Prospect Finder request ready for import
steps: Purchase credit bundle
Submit Prospect Finder request; import list preview and confirm
Verify credits deducted per pricing tier; try import exceeding balance
expected_results: Balance increases post-webhook
Deduction matches record types; audit entries for purchase and deduction
Exceeding balance blocked with clear error; no negative balance

requirements.implementation_notes: Store versions with immutable IDs; compute diffs server-side.

requirements.title: Authentication and session security
requirements.description: Email/password auth with strong policy, secure sessions, and password reset flow.
acceptance_criteria: Given password policy, when weak password is set, then it is rejected per policy.
Given session cookies, when transmitted, then they are HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax or Strict.
Given password reset, when requested, then tokenized flow expires and is audited.

test_cases.title: Permission matrix enforcement across roles
test_cases.description: Verify CRUD and approval rights per role per matrix.
preconditions: Users exist for all roles in same org; sample content present
steps: Advisor attempts to publish without approval
Compliance edits and approves content
Admin views analytics for all users; Advisor views own only
expected_results: Advisor blocked from publishing pre-approval
Compliance can edit/approve; status updates recorded
Admin sees org-wide analytics; Advisor sees own data only

test_cases.title: Row-level security: cross-tenant isolation
test_cases.description: Users cannot access other organizations’ data.
preconditions: Org A and Org B exist; user U_A (Org A) logged in; content and leads exist in both orgs
steps: Directly navigate to Org B resource IDs via URL/API
Attempt search/filter to surface cross-tenant data
Inspect network responses for foreign records
expected_results: All attempts return 403/404 with no data leakage
No foreign records appear; audit entries for denied access

test_cases.title: PII access logging and least privilege
test_cases.description: Access to PII is logged and restricted by role/tenant.
preconditions: Leads with PII exist; multiple roles available
steps: Advisor views own lead PII
Advisor attempts to view another advisor’s lead
Compliance views org-wide but read-only PII (where applicable)
Review PII access logs
expected_results: Unauthorized access blocked
Every PII read logged with actor/time/object
Compliance access follows policy; no edits unless allowed

test_cases.title: Analytics visibility respects role and tenant
test_cases.description: Dashboards filter by organization and role permissions.
preconditions: Org A and Org B with data; Admin/Advisor/Compliance users exist
steps: Admin Org A views analytics (org-wide)
Advisor Org A views analytics (own-only)
Cross-tenant URL/API attempts to fetch Org B analytics
expected_results: Admin sees only Org A aggregated data
Advisor sees own metrics only
Cross-tenant access blocked (403/404); no leakage

requirements.implementation_notes: Strong password validation, account lockout, registration redirect, deactivated-user denial, subscription gating, access-token refresh, and inactivity logout are verified with registration-security.json, account-lock.json, deactivated-user-login.json, subscription-gate.json, session-refresh.json, and idle-logout.json. The requirement remains in progress because the current auth implementation is bearer-token/localStorage based rather than secure session cookies, and the reset-token expiry/audit path still needs explicit end-to-end evidence.
